role: IRIAF Brigadier General and fighter pilot; killed by friendly fire during Iran-Iraq War
bio:
Abbas Babaei was an Iranian Brigadier General and fighter pilot in the Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force ([Artesh](/orgs/artesh)) during the [Iran-Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war). Born in Qazvin, he trained as a fighter pilot in the United States after graduating high school in 1969. He piloted Northrop F-5 aircraft and became commander of the 8th Tactical Air Base in 1981. Described as "the mastermind of IRIAF's capability to keep its F-14 fleet intact," Babaei was instrumental in maintaining Iran's air superiority during the war despite the post-revolution loss of US technical support.

On 6 August 1987, Babaei was killed when his F-5B aircraft was struck by a 23mm round from an Iranian air defense system operated by the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) over Sardasht airspace — a case of friendly fire. The pilot, Major Ali Mohammad Naderi, managed to land the aircraft, but Babaei died within ten minutes from his injuries.

sources.title: In 1980, Friendly Fire Was One of the Biggest Killers of Iranian Fighter Pilots

editorial_notes: Born 5 Dec 1950 Qazvin. Killed 6 Aug 1987 by IRGC friendly fire over Sardasht. Trained in US pre-revolution. Key to F-14 fleet survival.
